mechanism_narrative: Lower federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and discount future equity cash flows, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding one percentage point signals acute labor market distress that immediately suppresses consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of triggered instances despite the negative signal.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based private demand recovery, which sustains corporate earnings growth and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series reverts to trend within 30 days.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indices reflect improving manufacturing capacity utilization that directly boosts corporate profit margins, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases coincide with negative 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of observations during recovery phases.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth expands household income and aggregate consumption, which directly supports equity valuations and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC for PAYEMS growth remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation print exceeding half a percentage point triggers immediate market repricing of future central bank tightening, compressing equity multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX generates positive 5-day returns in > 70% of triggered instances despite the inflation threshold breach.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between quarterly GDP contraction and monthly retail sales decline confirms a synchronized demand collapse that forces earnings downgrades, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The negative interaction signal fails to predict negative 20-day returns when either series stabilizes within 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained industrial production growth during a post-crisis normalization phase indicates successful supply chain adaptation, which supports corporate revenue expansion and yields positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 60-day returns when macro volatility (VIX > 25) persists for more than 30 days.
